title: An effective high-molecular weight DNA extraction protocol for herbivorous insects containing sequestered plant compounds
author: Goldberg, Jay K
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Herbivorous insects often sequester defensive compounds obtained from their host plants. These compounds can co-precipitate with nucleic acids, making their isolation for sequencing purposes difficult. Herein, we present an extraction protocol that is capable of isolating high-quality high-molecular weight (HMW) DNA from insect tissue samples. Our protocol is based on well-established methods for extraction of HMW DNA from plant tissue samples that feature a CTAB (Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide) buffer and chloroform phase separation.
steps_raw: Herbivorous insects often sequester defensive compounds obtained from their host plants. These compounds can co-precipitate with nucleic acids, making their isolation for sequencing purposes difficult. Herein, we present an extraction protocol that is capable of isolating high-quality high-molecular weight (HMW) DNA from insect tissue samples. Our protocol is based on well-established methods for extraction of HMW DNA from plant tissue samples that feature a CTAB (Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide) buffer and chloroform phase separation.
